segments.text: Do you know this bright red road in India is saving thousands of lives, but it was not made for humans. You might think this red paint is to look cool,
but paint doesn't stop speeding cars in a forest. It's actually a tabletop marking. The surface is raised by exactly 5 mm. But why not just use the
normal speed bumps? See, on a fast highway, a big speed bump can cause accidents. It's too dangerous. So,
Madhya Pradesh use psychology instead of concrete. This design works on two psychological and physical principles.
First, visual cue. The red color grabs your eye instantly and then haptic feedback. The tiny 5 mm bumps creates a loud rumbling sound and vibration inside
your car. It forces you to naturally slow down which will prevent animal accidents. A simple design change which you would barely have noticed is keeping the wildlife
